rulings.Case Title: M. TODD JENKS VS. DLA PIPER RUDNICK GRAY CARY US LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition To Confirm Arb Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT DLA PIPER LLP's Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. The motion is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: M. TODD JENKS VS. DLA PIPER RUDNICK GRAY CARY US LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Staying Proceedings On Defts Dla Piper'S Petition To Confirm Arb Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF M. JENKS's Motion For Order Staying Proceedings On Defendant Dla Piper'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. The motion is denied. A stay is not warranted. This Court was the first to assert jurisdiction over the subject matter. See Mave Enterprises, Inc. v. Travelers Indemnity Co. (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 1408. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: M. TODD JENKS VS. DLA PIPER RUDNICK GRAY CARY US LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Demurrer To Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF M. JENKS' Demurrer To Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. The demurrer is overruled. A demurrer is not a proper response to a petition to confirm arbitration award. In addition, defendant has standing to bring the petition as the successor in interest. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL BYKHOVSKY et al VS. WILLIAM PORTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses From X-Deft Walsh To (1) Request For Production Of Documents And (2)Special Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014, Line 2, CROSS COMPLAINANT WILLIAM PORTER's Motion To Compel Further Responses From cross-defendant Walsh To (1) Request For Production Of Documents And (2)Special Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions. Granted as to Special Interrogatories nos. 10, 12, 17 - 19, 29 - 31, 35, 39, 40, 41 44 and 46. Denied as to all other Special Interrogatories and Requests for Production (RFP). RFP nos. 27 - 752 are overbroad and seek information into party assets that are not fundamental to the case. (CCP § 2031.030.) RFP no. 72 and Special Interrogatories nos. 3, 4, 6 - 9 relate to matters of public record that are equally available to the propounding party and seek information into party assets that are not fundamental to the case. Special Interrogatories nos. 17 - 19, 29 - 31 pertain to Skylake Development, LLC and Skylake Real Estate, Inc., who are both parties to the Complaint and Cross-Complaint, and Mr. Walsh shall provide further responses as to the information regarding these entities and his participation in these entities. Special Interrogatories nos. 16, 20, 24, 28 are too vague and tenuous to support an intrusion into the financial affairs of non-parties. (Britt v. Sup.Ct. (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844,855.) Special Interrogatories nos. 10, 12, 35, 39, 40, 41, 44, 46 are sufficiently specific and are routine in nature. Within 10 days of this order, further responses shall be served. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014, Line 15, DEFENDANTs ALYCE LEE and THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Motion For Summary Ajudication. The Court takes the motion off calendar as moot. On January 24, 2013, Plaintiff filed a third amended complaint that moots the instant motion. See State Compensation Insurance Fund v. Sup. Crt. (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1130-1131. Defendant may renew this motion or file a new motion for summary judgment/adjudication directed to the operative pleading. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: HENRIETTA MARTIN VS. DAVID DEMO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Defendants David Demo And Susan Demo'S Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Special Interrogatories, And Requests For Production Of Documents; P/A; Request For Sanctions Against Defendants And Their Attorney Of Record James Treppa; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014, Line 1, Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Defendants David Demo And Susan Demo's Further Responses To Plaintiff's Special Interrogatories, And Requests For Production Of Documents; P/A; Request For Sanctions Against Defendants And Their Attorney Of Record James Treppa Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: parties to appear after meeting and conferring to narrow the scope of the discovery requests at issue. The terms "concerning or relating" are very broad, however, the discovery is addressed to issues relevant to the subject matter of the action. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TBF FINANCIAL, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY VS. STEVEN J. BRATTESANI, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF TBF FINANCIAL, LLC's HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. Denied. Claimant is a judgment debtor to the Amended Judgment issued on January 10, 2012. On September 17, 2013, the Court issued an order adding Steven J. Brattesani, D.D.S., as an individual, as a judgment debtor. Claimant has not demonstrated that he needs the levied funds from Wells Fargo Bank in the amount of $ 105.57 for the support of Claimant and his dependents. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: RICHARD TOM VS. JEREMY D BLANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANT JEREMY BLANK's MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion to strike denied in part and granted in part. The motion to strike is denied as to punitive damages. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to state a claim for malice, oppression or fraud arising out of a breach of fiduciary duty. The motion to strike is granted as to paragraph 15 without leave to amend. The motion to strike is denied as to paragraphs 17, 21, 22, 24, and 28 as they do not conflict with the court's order on April 19, 2013 and are not irrelevant, false, or improper. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANT JEREMY BLANK's DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part. The demurrer is overruled as to the first cause of action. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts alleging breach of fiduciary duty in relation to defendant's rate increase without client consent. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as it relates to the second, third, and fourth causes of action. rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014, Line 5, PLAINTIFFS HELGA HORNER, JOHN TOBELER'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Defendant Coup D'Etat, Inc. To Further Respond To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, Numbers 49-67 Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied. As it appears that despite the objections, defendants have produced all responsive documents and therefore an order should reflect the foregoing and there is no need to compel as the order will reflect that all documents that are responsive to requests 49-67 have been produced. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: AVENTURA LIMOUSINE & TRANSPORTATION INC. VS. UNPARALLELED PRODUCTIONS INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014, Line 8, CROSS DEFENDANT AVENTURA LIMOUSINE & TRANSPORTATION INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled as to the breach of contract cause of action. Defendant sufficiently alleges damage and the sham doctrine does not apply because there is no inconsistency between the original and amended cross-complaint. The demurrer to the fraud cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. The cause of action still lacks the requisite particularity. Defendant has leave to allege in good faith the source of the representation, whether it was written or oral, and when it occurred. The allegations in paragraph 14 pertain to when the transportation troubles arose, not the original misrepresentations. There is no proper motion to strike on calendar. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER TAN VS. NOUR EL HOUDA TANVERAKUL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014, Line 9, CROSS DEFENDANT JENNIFER TAN's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustained in part and overruled in part. Cross-Defendant's request for judicial notice is denied. As to the first cause of action for defamation, the demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend. The First Amended Cross-Complaint ("FACC") does not plead facts that establish that the statements were reasonably understood by a third person to be about Cross-Plaintiff or that the third person reasonably understood the statement to damage Cross-Plaintiff's business reputation. (See Regalia v. Nethercutt Collection (2009) 172 Cal.App.4th 361, 368; "to be actionable per se, a defamatory statement must tend 'directly' to injure the person defamed in respect to his office, profession, trade or business.) As to the second cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress, the demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend. The FACC does not plead facts that establish Cross-Defendant engaged in conduct "so outrageous in character, and so extreme in degree, as to go beyond all possible bounds of decency, and to be regarded as atrocious, and utterly intolerable in a civilized community." (Cochran v. Cochran (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th 488, 496.) As to the third cause of action for intentional interference with prospective economic advantage, the demurrer is overruled. The FACC pleads facts establishing each element. (See Hughes v. Pair (2009) 46 Cal.4th 1035, 1050-51; FACC, 27, 62-71.) As to the fourth cause of action for violation of business and professions code sec. 17200 et seq., the demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend. The FACC does not adequately plead an "unlawful" business practice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ARSEL ZAPET ALVARADO VS. WORLD CAMERA, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Pltff To Further Respond And Produce Electronically Stored Information And To Impose Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 4, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANT WORLD CAMERA, INC.'s Motino To Compel Plaintiff To Further Respond And Produce Electronically Stored Information And To Impose Monetary Sanctions. Denied, without prejudice. The requested relief is premature. Pursuant to the agreement of the parties, Plaintiff is producing her redacted phone records in response to Defendant's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Requests for Production of Documents. These records may provide a basis for a motion directed to the ESI on the phone itself, or they may cut against putting the parties to that expense and inconvenience. After reviewing the phone records, Defendant may file a motion to produce ESI if warranted. Defendant is advised that cost-sharing for the production of ESI may be appropriate since it will require technical expertise to extract the ESI and segregate relevant material from irrelevant material. Monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
